title: Agent runners variants manifest
description: Machine-readable list of install overlays under variants/. The portal provisioner threads the chosen variant id into the cloud-init line as AGENT_RUNNER, which install.sh / base/run.sh resolve to a variants/<name>/ directory. The portal also vendors this file as the single source of truth for variant display data (kind, role-centric name/description, dep-chip fingerprint).

version.description: Manifest format version. Bump when the shape of an entry changes in a non-additive way.

default.description: Variant name install.sh / base/run.sh fall through to when AGENT_RUNNER is unset. MUST match one of variants[].name.

name.description: Slug matching the variants/<name>/ directory. Threaded into cloud-init as AGENT_RUNNER.

title.description: Short install-oriented label (what the overlay installs).

description.description: One-paragraph explanation of what the overlay adds, drops, and which scenario it targets.

hooks.description: Names of hook scripts the variant ships (e.g. 'early-setup', 'pre-services', 'post-services'). The corresponding file must exist at variants/<name>/<hook>.sh. Omit a hook by leaving it out of the array (base/run.sh skips missing hooks silently).

default.description: Convenience marker mirroring the top-level `default` field. Only one variant should set this to true.

kind.description: Capability class. As of the component model there is a single 'base' variant (ubuntu-claude-code); capabilities are layered as components (components.json), so dep chips are component-derived. Legacy values (ext = extension bridge, noext = SB server + native browser, bare = no SB runtime) are retained for back-compat display of pre-component rows.

display.description: Role-centric product display used as the fleet-list / detail-page fallback when a row has no profile (legacy / unknown slug).

description.description: One-line stack description, e.g. 'SideButton MCP + Extension'.

deps.description: Dep-chip fingerprint the portal renders for this variant — the components it installs. This is the authority for what an agent of this variant shows as installed.

live.description: True ⇒ the chip shows a process-driven status dot; false ⇒ a static 'installed' declaration.

processKey.description: Which process signal drives the live dot (live chips only).

versionKey.description: Which dependency_versions field provides the version label.
